name: Grilled Slapshot Salad
recipeIngredient: 3 large garlic cloves, pressed (or 4 small garlic cloves, or 1½ teaspoons minced garlic)
3 medium zucchini (or 4 small zucchinis), sliced on the bias into ¼-inch slices
1 sweet onion, sliced into ¼-inch pieces
2 ears corn (fresh or frozen; thaw if necessary)
1 tablespoon sherry vinaigrette (see Exhibition Salad recipe)
1 tablespoon chopped fresh basil (about 1 to 2 teaspoons)
recipeInstructions:
1. Whisk together the olive oil, salt, pepper, and garlic in a bowl.
2. Divide the mixture between two 9x13-inch glass baking pans.
3. Lightly coat the zucchini slices with the oil-garlic mixture using your hands.
4. Trim the stems from the mushrooms, slice the onion into ¼-inch pieces, and place them into one of the pans. Mix gently so all vegetables are lightly coated.
5. Preheat the grill and oven to 400°F (200°C).
6. Place the zucchini slices on the grill and cook for no more than 30 seconds on one side only. Transfer back to their pan, cooked side up, and into the oven for no longer than 10 minutes.
7. Briefly grill the mushrooms, onion slices, and corn until they have grill marks but are not fully cooked (a few minutes).
8. Remove the onion slices and mushrooms; set aside to cool.
9. Cut off the kernels from each ear of corn on a cutting surface perpendicular to the grain.
10. Combine the zucchini slices, mushrooms, onion slices, and corn kernels in a bowl.
11. Pour the sherry vinaigrette over the vegetables and stir in the fresh basil. Serve immediately or refrigerate for no more than 1 hour.
12. Serves 4.
